idea: Event-driven deployment tracker with API, worker, and Redis queues
why_this_project_matters: This project models a multi-service container platform with asynchronous work, internal networking, and isolated build artifacts.
architecture: A FastAPI service receives deployment events and writes them to Redis-backed queues. A worker container consumes queued tasks and records deployment outcomes. Docker Compose creates a private network, starts Redis first, and exposes only the API port to callers.
steps: Build the API and worker images separately to keep responsibilities clear.
Start Redis, then verify the API container can reach it over the Compose network.
Submit a deployment event through the API and confirm the worker consumes the queue.
Add persistent volumes or external queueing when moving beyond local development.
Extend the worker with retries, backoff, and structured logging for production readiness.
